title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
summary: Alane Suhr discusses agency in language, examining how words convey meaning, what language models fundamentally are, and arguing that humans (not AI systems) retain agency in language use. The talk covers instruction tuning, RLHF, and semantic connotations of the term AI.
writeup: Alane Suhr's Compile 26 talk examines agency in language: how words convey meaning, what language models fundamentally are, and the claim that humans, not AI systems, retain agency in language use. The discussion works through instruction tuning and RLHF as the mechanisms shaping model behavior, and questions the semantic connotations carried by the term AI. The central takeaway is a framing argument that locates agency with people rather than the systems they build.
